title: Bishop Cuts the Window
setup: Your rook on **e7** is one step from the back rank, and the bishop on **b4** rakes the **a3-f8** diagonal — including {{f8}}.
hint: Slide the rook to the 8th rank — your bishop on **b4** already controls the {{f8}} escape square.
explanation:
## The Idea

A **rook + bishop mating net**. The pawn shield blocks {{g8}}'s usual escape via h7/g7; the bishop on **b4** plugs the only diagonal escape on {{f8}}.

Play {{Re8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{f8}} — controlled by the bishop on **b4** (b4-c5-d6-e7-f8 diagonal — wait, e7 has the rook! Actually after Re8, e7 is empty so the bishop's diagonal is **a3-f8** running b4-c5-d6-e7-f8, which now has nothing on it)
- {{h8}} — covered by the rook on e8 (8th rank)
- {{f7}}, {{g7}}, {{h7}} — all blocked by Black's own pawns
- The king cannot capture the rook: e8 is too far from g8

> **Takeaway:** A bishop pointing at the long diagonal is the perfect partner for a back-rank rook. Together they cover both ends of the 8th rank — the rook from one side, the bishop slicing the other.
